title: platydesma spathulatum var. pallidum
intro: Platydesma spathulatum var. pallidum, also known as the 'Kahili Ginger', is a plant species within the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Platydesma spathulatum var. pallidum include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve nausea and fever, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as an infusion, made by steeping the leaves in hot water, or as a tincture, extracted using a solvent like ethanol.
